Beyond Week 4: You know the system. Every Sunday, pick three proteins, two bases, and two sauces. Spend 90 minutes. Eat well all week. You are not following a plan anymore — you have built a habit.

bonuses.title: BONUS 1 — The 21-Question Protein Profile Quiz
body_lines: Answer honestly. Circle your answer for each question. Tally your score at the end.
LIFESTYLE
How many times per week do you currently cook at home?
a) 0–1 times (1 pt) | b) 2–3 times (2 pts) | c) 4+ times (3 pts)
How would you describe your weekday schedule?
a) Extremely busy, barely time to eat (1 pt) | b) Busy but manageable (2 pts) | c) Flexible with time to cook (3 pts)
How much time are you realistically willing to spend on Sunday prep?
a) Under 60 minutes (1 pt) | b) 60–90 minutes (2 pts) | c) 90+ minutes (3 pts)
Do you have basic kitchen equipment (baking sheet, skillet, large pot)?
a) No (1 pt) | b) Some of it (2 pts) | c) Yes, fully equipped (3 pts)
How comfortable are you in the kitchen?
a) Beginner — I follow recipes closely (1 pt) | b) Comfortable with basic cooking (2 pts) | c) Confident, can improvise (3 pts)
FOOD PREFERENCES
Do you eat meat, fish, and eggs?
a) No, vegetarian or vegan (1 pt) | b) Some — I have preferences (2 pts) | c) Yes, no restrictions (3 pts)
How adventurous are you with new flavors?
a) I stick to what I know (1 pt) | b) Open to trying new things (2 pts) | c) I love variety (3 pts)
Do you enjoy meal prepping and eating the same meal multiple days in a row?
a) I dislike it (1 pt) | b) It is fine for 2–3 days (2 pts) | c) No problem (3 pts)
How important is it to you that your meals taste restaurant-quality?
a) Very — I easily abandon food that is not satisfying (1 pt) | b) Somewhat (2 pts) | c) Not very — function over flavor (3 pts)
Do you have a history of food allergies or intolerances?
a) Yes, multiple restrictions (1 pt) | b) One or two (2 pts) | c) None (3 pts)
DIET HISTORY
How many times have you attempted a structured diet or meal plan?
a) More than 5 times (1 pt) | b) 2–4 times (2 pts) | c) 1 time or never (3 pts)
What is the longest you have ever stuck with a diet?
a) Less than 2 weeks (1 pt) | b) 2–8 weeks (2 pts) | c) More than 2 months (3 pts)
What is the primary reason your past diets have failed?
a) Willpower or cravings (1 pt) | b) Lack of time (2 pts) | c) Boredom with the food (3 pts)
Have you ever used a meal prep system before?
a) No (1 pt) | b) Yes, briefly (2 pts) | c) Yes, regularly (3 pts)
GOALS AND ACTIVITY
How much weight are you looking to lose?
a) 30+ lbs (1 pt) | b) 15–30 lbs (2 pts) | c) Under 15 lbs (3 pts)
How would you describe your current activity level?
a) Sedentary — mostly sitting (1 pt) | b) Lightly active — some walking (2 pts) | c) Active — exercise 3+ days per week (3 pts)
Do you currently track calories or macros?
a) No, never (1 pt) | b) Occasionally (2 pts) | c) Yes, regularly (3 pts)
What is your primary health goal?
a) Lose weight (1 pt) | b) Lose weight and build better habits (2 pts) | c) Lose weight and improve fitness (3 pts)
MENTAL AND EMOTIONAL
How confident are you that you will follow this plan for 30 days?
a) Low confidence (1 pt) | b) Moderate confidence (2 pts) | c) High confidence (3 pts)
How do you handle a "bad day" where you eat off-plan?
a) I usually give up entirely (1 pt) | b) I feel guilty but continue (2 pts) | c) I get back on track the next meal (3 pts)
How important is seeing results quickly to staying motivated?
a) Very — I need results in week one or I lose motivation (1 pt) | b) Somewhat (2 pts) | c) I can stay patient for 2–4 weeks (3 pts)
YOUR SCORE:
21–35 points — BEGINNER

Start with Week 1 of the plan and follow it exactly. Do not skip ahead. Your goal this month is not perfection — it is consistency. Complete your Sunday prep session every week, even if it is only 45 minutes and only three things. Focus on the Breakfasts, Snacks, and Soups chapters. Meals that reheat easily and require no assembly keep things simple.
36–50 points — INTERMEDIATE

Start with Week 1 but you can move through the weeks more quickly. By Week 2, try introducing two new protein sources you have not used before. You are comfortable in the kitchen — trust that and experiment with the sauces in Chapter 8. The Mix-and-Match Matrix in Week 4 is made for you.
51–63 points — ADVANCED

You already know how to cook and meal prep. What you need is variety and a smarter structure. Jump to Week 2 and go straight for the more complex recipes — Creamy Tuscan Chicken, Garlic Shrimp & Zucchini Noodles, Chicken Tikka-Style Skillet. Build your own plan from Week 1 using the Mix-and-Match Matrix. Your focus is on the sauce chapter and ensuring all six protein sources rotate through your week.

bonuses.title: BONUS 2 — 4-Week Home Workout Plan
body_lines: No equipment. No gym. 15 minutes per session. Three sessions per week.

Why exercise matters alongside this plan: protein preserves muscle. Exercise builds it. The combination means your body composition changes faster than diet alone — you are not just losing weight, you are reshaping where the weight comes from. These workouts are designed to complement the meal plan, not replace anything. Do three sessions per week on non-consecutive days.
BEGINNER LEVEL — Weeks 1 & 2
30 seconds work / 20 seconds rest / 3 rounds / 15 minutes total
Session A:
Wall sit (30 sec)
Modified push-ups on knees (30 sec)
Glute bridges (30 sec)
Standing marching in place (30 sec)
Bird-dog — alternate sides (30 sec)
Session B:
Step-touch side to side (30 sec)
Standing leg raises (30 sec)
Seated leg lifts (30 sec)
Superman back extensions (30 sec)
Standing calf raises (30 sec)
INTERMEDIATE LEVEL — Weeks 2 & 3
40 seconds work / 20 seconds rest / 3 rounds / 18 minutes total
Session A:
Bodyweight squats (40 sec)
Push-ups (40 sec)
Reverse lunges alternating legs (40 sec)
Plank hold (40 sec)
Mountain climbers (40 sec)
Session B:
Jump squats (40 sec)
Tricep dips on a chair (40 sec)
Side lunges alternating (40 sec)
Hip thrusts (40 sec)
High knees (40 sec)
ADVANCED LEVEL — Weeks 3 & 4
45 seconds work / 15 seconds rest / 3 rounds / 18 minutes total
Session A:
Jump squats (45 sec)
Push-up to downward dog (45 sec)
Bulgarian split squats (45 sec each leg)
Plank to alternating shoulder tap (45 sec)
Burpees (45 sec)
Session B:
Speed skaters (45 sec)
Decline push-ups — feet on couch (45 sec)
Lateral band walks — no band needed, just slow controlled steps (45 sec)
Single-leg glute bridge (45 sec each side)
Tuck jumps (45 sec)

bonuses.title: BONUS 3 — High-Protein Eating Out Guide
body_lines: Staying on plan when life happens.
The plan works best at home. But life means restaurants, work lunches, and road trips. Here is how to stay on track at the 10 most common chains in the US.
McDonald's
✅ Order: Egg McMuffin + side of scrambled eggs (36g protein together) | Grilled Chicken Sandwich, no sauce
❌ Avoid: McFlurry, French Fries as a main side, Crispy Chicken (fried)
Protein hack: Order two Egg McMuffins for a quick 28g breakfast under $5.
Chipotle
✅ Order: Burrito bowl with double chicken or steak, black beans, fajita veggies, fresh salsa, Greek yogurt instead of sour cream (40g+ protein)
❌ Avoid: Chips and guacamole as your primary calories, cheese overload
Protein hack: Double meat always. The extra cost is worth it. Get the bowl, not the burrito — saves 300 calories.
Panera Bread
✅ Order: You Pick 2 — Ten Vegetable Soup + Chicken Noodle Soup | Fuji Apple Chicken Salad (full size)
❌ Avoid: Mac & Cheese bowls, pastries, lemonade
Protein hack: Ask for an extra side of chicken on any salad.
Subway
✅ Order: Oven-Roasted Chicken or Turkey Breast on whole wheat, load vegetables, no mayo (30g+ protein in a footlong)
❌ Avoid: Meatball marinara, footlong cookies, creamy sauces
Protein hack: Double the protein. It costs $1–2 extra and nearly doubles your protein count.
Chick-fil-A
✅ Order: Grilled Chicken Sandwich (no sauce) + Grilled Nuggets 8pc | Grilled Market Salad
❌ Avoid: Waffle fries as your only side, Chick-fil-A Sauce (280 calories), milkshakes
Protein hack: Grilled Nuggets (25g protein, 130 calories) are one of the best fast food protein options in the country.
Olive Garden
✅ Order: Herb-Grilled Salmon | Chicken Margherita | Soup and salad as lighter option
❌ Avoid: Unlimited breadsticks (they will derail you before your meal arrives), Fettuccine Alfredo
Protein hack: Ask for the grilled chicken upgrade on any pasta to add 20g protein while keeping the dish satisfying.
Applebee's
✅ Order: Cedar Salmon | 7 oz House Sirloin with steamed vegetables | Pepper-Crusted Sirloin & Shrimp
❌ Avoid: Full-size loaded appetizers as your meal, anything described as "crispy" or "loaded"
Protein hack: Order from the "under 550 calories" menu. Most of those options are surprisingly high in protein.
Starbucks
✅ Order: Egg White & Roasted Red Pepper Egg Bites (2 bites = 20g protein) | Spinach, Feta & Cage-Free Egg White Wrap (20g protein) | Protein Box with hardboiled eggs
❌ Avoid: Grande Caramel Frappuccino (66g sugar), Banana Bread, Birthday Cake Pops
Protein hack: Pair two Egg Bites with a plain cold brew for a 20g protein breakfast under 300 calories.
The Cheesecake Factory
✅ Order: SkinnyLicious Grilled Salmon | SkinnyLicious Chicken Soft Tacos | Grilled Turkey Burger on lettuce wrap
❌ Avoid: The famous portions — everything here is 2–3 servings. Ask for a to-go box immediately and pack half before eating.
Protein hack: The SkinnyLicious menu is legitimate — use it every time.
Domino's
✅ Order: Pacific Veggie or Grilled Chicken pizza, thin crust, 2 slices | Chicken Wings (plain, not fried) as a protein addition
❌ Avoid: Extra cheese, stuffed crust, extra-large combo deals that encourage over-ordering
Protein hack: Two thin crust slices with grilled chicken + a side of wings = 35g+ protein and still under 600 calories.

bonuses.title: BONUS 4 — Progress Tracker & Measurement Log
body_lines: The scale is one data point. Not the only one.

Weight alone does not tell the full story. Muscle weighs more than fat. A week of weightlifting can make the scale go up while your body is actually improving. This tracker measures what actually matters.
Weekly Weigh-In and Measurement Log
Fill in every Sunday morning, before eating, after using the bathroom.
| Week | Weight | Waist | Hips | Thigh | Arm |
| Start |  |  |  |  |  |
| Total Change |  |  |  |  |  |
Take measurements in inches. Measure waist at the narrowest point, hips at the widest point, thigh at the mid-thigh, and arm at the bicep.
Daily Energy & Hunger Level Log
Rate each day from 1 (very low) to 5 (very high).
| Mon | Tue | Wed | Thu | Fri | Sat | Sun |
| Energy Level |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Hunger Level |  |  |  |  |  |  |

What to look for: As the weeks progress, hunger levels should decrease and energy levels should increase. If both stay high in week 2, increase portion sizes slightly. If energy drops consistently in week 3, make sure you are hitting your protein targets every day.
Weekly Win Journal
Every Sunday, write one win that has nothing to do with the scale.
| Week | My non-scale win this week |
| Week 1 |  |
| Week 2 |  |
| Week 3 |  |
| Week 4 |  |

Examples: "I had energy after dinner for the first time in months." "I said no to the office donuts and did not feel deprived." "I fit into jeans I have not worn in two years." "I cooked every meal this week." These are the wins that tell you the system is working.

conclusion.title: Conclusion
conclusion.subtitle: You Have the System Now
paragraphs: Thirty days ago, or maybe just a few pages ago, you were standing in the kitchen at 7 PM wondering what to eat and feeling like you were failing at something that should not be this hard.
It was never that hard. You just needed the right system.

What you have in this book is not a diet. It is a blueprint for how a week of food actually works when it is built around your real life. One Sunday. Two hours. One week of decisions already made. Proteins that rotate so you never hit that wall of boredom that kills every other plan.

The people who succeed with this system are not the ones who love cooking. They are not the ones with the most willpower or the best genetics. They are the ones who show up on Sunday, even when they do not feel like it, and do the prep. Thirty minutes into it they are always glad they did.
You know how to do that now.

The 30-day plan gets you started. The Mix-and-Match Matrix keeps you going past day 30. The sauce chapter makes sure you never get bored. The bonus toolkit makes sure you see progress on more than just the scale.
Every tool you need is in this book. What happens next is up to you — and based on the fact that you read all the way to the last page, you already know you are going to do it this time.
See you in week five.
